params.title: One Number Per Field. *That's* The Whole Idea.
params.kicker: Meet the KurimaScore — the instrument the last two posts were building towards.

slides.alt_text: A green tractor beside a tall maize stand under a brooding sky. Large yellow serif text reads: one number per field. That's the whole idea.

params.title: Your Field, In One Read.
params.body: One score from 0 to 100, refreshed as the satellite passes. Not a grade — a prompt to look in the right place.

ui.action: Scout the north edge before you irrigate the whole block.

slides.alt_text: Tomato rows inside a tunnel greenhouse. A dark panel shows a KurimaScore readout: Block 4, maize, 12.4 hectares. Score 72, band reads holding, north edge slipping. Action: scout the north edge before you irrigate the whole block.

slides.alt_text: A green tractor beside a maize stand. Three columns explain what the KurimaScore folds in: water, canopy stress and moisture read before the wilt; vigour, whether the crop is still climbing week on week; variation, which corner is sliding while the rest holds.

params.question: If every field had *one* honest number this week — which would you check first?
params.cta: FOLLOW KURIMASENSE — SCORES FOR YOUR FIELDS ARE COMING

slides.alt_text: A young woman farmer in a straw hat leans on a wooden rail, looking ahead. Text asks: if every field had one honest number this week, which would you check first? A yellow banner says follow KurimaSense, scores for your fields are coming.

caption:
We spent two posts on the problem. Here is the instrument.

The KurimaScore is one number per field, 0 to 100, refreshed as the satellite passes overhead. It is not a grade on your farming — it is a prompt that tells you where to point your attention this week.

Under that one number sits the work the eye can't do quickly: canopy stress and moisture, whether vigour is still climbing, and which corner of the block is sliding while the rest holds. You get the read; you make the call.

That is the whole design philosophy. Not a dashboard you have to babysit. One honest number per field, early enough to matter.

Follow along — scores for your own fields are coming. Save this for the person who runs the fields with you.
first_comment: The KurimaScore is a composite read of publicly-sourced satellite signals — canopy temperature, vegetation vigour and within-field variation. The example readout here is illustrative, not a real client field: we never fabricate customer results. When we show a real score, it will be labelled as one.
rationale: The payoff post — the only product-led brief in the series (Operator Lens, the 20%). It earns the product mention because the two prior posts taught the problem. Hook is the product's design philosophy in one line. Arc: introduce the score, show a concrete but explicitly illustrative readout (feature UI), unpack the three signals it folds in (icon-row ties back to posts 1–2), and close on a save/follow CTA. First comment labels the example as illustrative, not a client result (compliance: no fabricated clients). British spelling, Zimbabwe furniture (blocks, hectares, irrigate, scouting).
